REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. MEET AND CONFER DECLARATION FILED CONCURRENTLY WITH THE MOTION DOES NOT SPECIFY ANY MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS AFTER APRIL 3, 2026 AUTOMATIC EXTENSION DECLARATION WAS FILED. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT ON APRIL 1, 2026 A STIPULATION RE FILING OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT HAS BEEN FILED. THE COURT HAS NEVER RECEIVED A HARD COURTESY COPY OF THE STIPULATION OR A HARD COPY OF THE ORDER ON STIPULATION FOR EXECUTION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDER TO MEET AND CONFER, AND IF UPON SUCH EFFORTS, THE PARTIES ARE TO STIPULATE TO THE FILING OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE STIPULATION MUST BE FILED AND COURTESY COPY OF THE STIPULATION AND ORDER MUST BE PROMPTLY DELIVERED TO THE COURT. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER NO LATER THAT JUNE 30, 2026. IF NECESSARY AND PROPER, DEFENDANT MAY RE-FILE THE DEMURRER THEREAFTER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT

is OFF CALENDR. See ruling on the demurrer.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

is OFF CALENDAR. Meet and Confer declaration filed concurrently with the motion does not specify any meet and confer efforts after April 3, 2026 automatic extension declaration was filed. The Court also notes that on April 1, 2026 a stipulation re filing of Second Amended Complaint has been filed. The Court has never received a hard courtesy copy of the stipulation or a hard copy of the order on stipulation for execution. The parties are order to meet and confer, and if upon such efforts, the parties are to stipulate to the filing of the Second Amended Complaint, the stipulation must be filed and courtesy copy of the stipulation and order must be promptly delivered to the Court. The parties shall meet and confer no later that June 30, 2026. If necessary and proper, Defendant may re-file the demurrer thereafter.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
